completeness: Missing from REQUIREMENTS list (ac-ids.txt): AC-1.3, AC-3.6, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.4. No Scenario Outline used for boundary cases (e.g., loan limit at 4 vs 5 vs 6, fine at 19 vs 20 days late). Negative ACs are well represented (3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.3, 5.2, 5.3, 6.3, 2.2, 2.4, 1.4).
alignment: Every scenario carries exactly one @AC-x.y tag (catalog.feature:16,23,30,36; lending.feature:16,23,30,37,44; reservations.feature:16,22,29,36,44; etc.)

Scenario names restate the AC in human-readable form: 'Reject borrowing more than 5 books' (lending.feature:24), 'Cannot pay an already paid fine' (fines.feature:28), 'Reject duplicate ISBN' (manage.feature:13). Background present in 5 of 6 features to reduce duplication. Not fully bidirectional (5 ACs untraced); no Outline Examples tagged per row.
declarative: Good: 'When Alice Johnson borrows "Dune"' (lending.feature:18); 'When the librarian lends...' style; 'Then "Dune" has 0 available copies' (lending.feature:21); 'Then the fine is marked as paid' (fines.feature:25)

Bad: persistent leakage of HTTP/JSON vocabulary — 'Then the response status is 200' (catalog.feature:19, returns.feature:20, etc.), 'Then the response includes unpaid_fines_cents of 350' (fines.feature:18 — JSON key), 'And the loan has a due_date' (lending.feature:20 — JSON key), 'Then the response status is 402' (lending.feature:41). Magic numbers used instead of named constants (e.g., '$1.50', '5 active loans', '$5.01' rather than '<loan limit>').
atomic: Atomic example: 'When Alice Johnson returns the loan' (returns.feature:19); 'And Bob Smith has reserved "Dune"' (returns.feature:42)

Compound exception: 'When the book is returned and Bob Smith is notified' (reservations.feature:40,49) — two actions in one step. 'When the librarian creates a book with title "The Hobbit", author "J.R.R. Tolkien", ISBN "978-0547928227", and 4 copies' (manage.feature:8) is parameter-rich but a single action.
